publishedAbstract: It is now possible to clone and restore volumes across different Ceph CSI storage classes. This helps in the following scenarios: * When your Ceph cluster has pools with multiple device classes or pools with different failure-domain rules, and you want to transition a persistent volume claim (PVC) to another. For example, you might want to take a volume snapshot of a PVC from rbd-ssd StorageClass, clone it to rbd-hdd, and delete the snapshot on the more expensive StorageClass. * When you want to take a snapshot of an image on a storage class that has lower failure-domain tolerance, and clone it to a StorageClass that has higher failure-domain tolerance. * When you are enforced to reduce storage footprint. * When you want to move from a storage class with replica 3 to replica 2 or replica 1 where you have your own high availability or underlying storage providing resiliency.
publishedTitle: Ability to clone and restore volumes across different Ceph CSI storage classes - Dev Preview
